headline: Strategic angle — Implement enhanced anti-fraud controls and board oversight to regain ESG index inclusion and reduce cost of capital by up to 40 bps (~₹80 (engine estimate)-100 Cr annualized, engine estimate) (of ₹997.1 Cr canonical event exposure)
what_matters: Significant ESG score gap emerges versus peers; risk of exclusion from DJSI and negative rating actions due to weak anti-corruption controls.
Severe reputational damage may impact customer retention and talent acquisition, as indicated by Edelman Trust Barometer correlations.

Triggers BRSR, GRI:205 (rationale: Triggered by any fraud disclosure, bribery allegation, insider trading investigation, or ED/CBI action. Also referenced for ISO 37001 certification.), GRI:207 (rationale: Triggered by GST demand notices, transfer-pricing disputes, tax-disclosure gaps, or related-party transaction investigation. Complements ESRS G1.), ESRS G1; SEBI scrutiny and possible trading restrictions; regulatory penalties up to ₹132.8 Cr (engine estimate).
action: Strategic opportunity: Implement enhanced anti-fraud controls and board oversight to regain ESG index inclusion and reduce cost of capital by up to 40 bps (~₹80 (engine estimate)-100 Cr annualized, engine estimate) (of ₹997.1 Cr canonical event exposure)
Competitive position: Material loss of trust versus ICICI Bank and YES Bank; likely to lose ESG index inclusion and face higher funding costs
